title: Kobresia bellardii
taxonomy: Kobresia bellardii is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae. This plant is a member of the genus Kobresia, which consists of about 20 species of sedge. Kobresia bellardii is native to the mountainous regions of the Northern Hemisphere, specifically in Asia and Europe. It is a type of low-growing, grass-like plant that thrives in wet environments. Kobresia bellardii is often found in areas with acidic soils and is an important component of alpine and subalpine ecosystems.

This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which provide various health benefits, including wound healing, fever reduction, and immune system support.
